title: Podospermum subulatum
taxonomy: Podospermum subulatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae, now more commonly known as the Asteraceae family. It is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae, within the Asteraceae family. The plant is native to South Africa and is characterized by its small, yellow or white flowers. Further classification includes its placement in the genus Podospermum, which contains several species of similar plants. Podospermum subulatum is a succulent plant that grows in dry areas, making it well-suited to its native environment.

intro: Podospermum subulatum, also known as the prickly lettuce or prickly sowthistle, is a biennial or perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) characterized by its prickly leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Podospermum subulatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues.
